description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by three wide, dark metal hoops that encircle the barrel at the top, middle, and bottom. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made from the same wood as the sides, with the top appearing to be securely closed. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the surface shows no labels or markings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near garages, often used for storage or decorative purposes.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with three dark metal hoops, bulging at the center and closed at the top. The surface is rustic, with no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical shape, three metal hoops, and the closed wooden top.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the curvature and the continuity of the metal hoops around the barrel.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the bulging center and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 2, with clear visibility of the wood grain and metal bands.
